main_paper.raw: {"methods":["VideoMV: a multi-view generation model fine-tuned from pre-trained video generative models","3D-Aware Denoising Sampling procedure: uses a feed-forward reconstruction module to obtain a global 3D model and incorporates rendered images from this model into the denoising loop","Camera embedding as a residual to the time embedding for each frame to connect multi-view images with object-centric videos"],"problems":["Generating multi-view images from text or single-image prompts for 3D content creation","Ensuring multi-view consistency in generated images","Reducing the domain gap and improving generalizability in multi-view generation models","Improving efficiency (reducing training time and computational resources) for multi-view generation"],"datasets":["A small high-quality 3D dataset (not named explicitly in the provided text)","Object-centric video datasets (used for pre-training video generative models)"],"metrics":["Image quality","Multi-view consistency","Training efficiency (GPU hours)"],"results":[{"metric":"Training efficiency (GPU hours for 24-view generation)","value":"4 GPU hours (VideoMV) vs 2300 GPU hours (MVDream for 4-view generation)"},{"metric":"Image quality and multi-view consistency","value":"VideoMV outperforms MVDream (Shi et al. 2023b)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to fine-tune off-the-shelf video generative models for multi-view synthesis, leveraging their temporal consistency for improved multi-view consistency.","Introduction of a novel 3D-Aware Denoising Sampling procedure that explicitly reconstructs a global 3D model and incorporates it into the denoising loop to enhance multi-view consistency.","Significantly improved training efficiency (4 GPU hours for 24 views) compared to state-of-the-art methods (thousands of GPU hours for fewer views).","Ability to generate dense (24) consistent views with comparable or better visual quality and consistency than previous methods.","The 3D rectification is performed at inference, allowing the use of pretrained 2D and video model priors for better generalizability."]}

methods: VideoMV: a multi-view generation model fine-tuned from pre-trained video generative models
3D-Aware Denoising Sampling procedure: uses a feed-forward reconstruction module to obtain a global 3D model and incorporates rendered images from this model into the denoising loop
Camera embedding as a residual to the time embedding for each frame to connect multi-view images with object-centric videos
problems: Generating multi-view images from text or single-image prompts for 3D content creation
Ensuring multi-view consistency in generated images
Reducing the domain gap and improving generalizability in multi-view generation models
Improving efficiency (reducing training time and computational resources) for multi-view generation
datasets: A small high-quality 3D dataset (not named explicitly in the provided text)
Object-centric video datasets (used for pre-training video generative models)

results.value: 4 GPU hours (VideoMV) vs 2300 GPU hours (MVDream for 4-view generation)

novelty_claims: First to fine-tune off-the-shelf video generative models for multi-view synthesis, leveraging their temporal consistency for improved multi-view consistency.
Introduction of a novel 3D-Aware Denoising Sampling procedure that explicitly reconstructs a global 3D model and incorporates it into the denoising loop to enhance multi-view consistency.
Significantly improved training efficiency (4 GPU hours for 24 views) compared to state-of-the-art methods (thousands of GPU hours for fewer views).
Ability to generate dense (24) consistent views with comparable or better visual quality and consistency than previous methods.
The 3D rectification is performed at inference, allowing the use of pretrained 2D and video model priors for better generalizability.

methods: VideoMV: a multi-view generation model fine-tuned from pre-trained video generative models

3D-Aware Denoising Sampling: a denoising strategy that incorporates a feed-forward reconstruction module to generate an explicit global 3D model and uses rendered images from this model in the denoising loop
Camera embedding as a residual to the time embedding for each frame to connect multi-view images with object-centric videos
problems: Generating multi-view images from text or single-image prompts for 3D content creation
Ensuring multi-view consistency in generated images
Reducing the domain gap and improving efficiency in multi-view generation training
datasets: A small high-quality 3D dataset (exact name not specified in the provided content)
Object-centric video datasets (general description, not specific names provided)
metrics: Image quality metrics (not specified, but likely include FID, IS, or similar)
Multi-view consistency metrics (not specified, but likely include LPIPS, SSIM, or similar)

results.value: 4 GPU hours (VideoMV) vs 2300 GPU hours (MVDream for 4-view generation)
VideoMV outperforms MVDream [63] (exact metric values not specified)

novelty_claims: First to fine-tune off-the-shelf video generative models for dense multi-view image generation, leveraging their temporal consistency for improved multi-view consistency.
Introduction of a novel 3D-Aware Denoising Sampling strategy that explicitly reconstructs a global 3D model and incorporates rendered images into the denoising loop to enhance multi-view consistency.

Significantly improved training efficiency (4 GPU hours for 24 views) compared to state-of-the-art methods (thousands of GPU hours for fewer views) while achieving superior or comparable visual quality and consistency.
Provides a fast way to create 3D assets represented by 3D Gaussians within a few seconds as a by-product.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Fine-tuning a pre-trained video diffusion model (EMU Video) with rendered multi-view videos from 100K 3D data to unlock multi-view generative capabilities","Generating a large-scale synthetic multi-view dataset (3M samples) using the fine-tuned video diffusion model and web-scale text prompts","Training a feed-forward 3D generative model (based on Large Reconstruction Model, LRM) on the synthetic multi-view dataset","Introducing training strategies to stabilize learning from large-scale synthetic data","Post-training finetuning of the 3D generative model with real 3D renderings"],"problems":["Scarcity and limited availability of high-quality 3D data for training foundation 3D generative models","Low-quality textures and geometric issues (e.g., floaters) in current 3D generative models","Need for scalable and efficient methods to generate high-quality 3D assets from limited 3D data"],"datasets":["Synthetic multi-view dataset generated by the fine-tuned video diffusion model (3 million samples)","Rendered multi-view videos from 100K 3D data (used for fine-tuning the video diffusion model and post-training finetuning)"],"metrics":["User preference (user study)","Automated metrics for 3D asset quality (not specified in detail)"],"results":[{"metric":"User preference","value":"Users preferred VFusion3D results over 90% of the time compared to current SOTA feed-forward 3D generative models"}],"novelty_claims":["First to leverage pre-trained video diffusion models as a scalable 3D data generator for training 3D generative models","Unlocking multi-view generative capabilities in video diffusion models through fine-tuning with 3D data","Demonstrating that synthetic multi-view data generated from video diffusion models can effectively train high-quality 3D generative models","Achieving state-of-the-art performance in feed-forward 3D asset generation from a single image, with significant user preference over existing methods"]}

methods: Fine-tuning a pre-trained video diffusion model (EMU Video) with rendered multi-view videos from 100K 3D data to unlock multi-view generative capabilities
Generating a large-scale synthetic multi-view dataset (3M samples) using the fine-tuned video diffusion model and web-scale text prompts
Training a feed-forward 3D generative model (based on Large Reconstruction Model, LRM) on the synthetic multi-view dataset
Introducing training strategies to stabilize learning from large-scale synthetic data
Post-training finetuning of the 3D generative model with real 3D renderings
problems: Scarcity and limited availability of high-quality 3D data for training foundation 3D generative models
Low-quality textures and geometric issues (e.g., floaters) in current 3D generative models
Need for scalable and efficient methods to generate high-quality 3D assets from limited 3D data
datasets: Synthetic multi-view dataset generated by the fine-tuned video diffusion model (3 million samples)
Rendered multi-view videos from 100K 3D data (used for fine-tuning the video diffusion model and post-training finetuning)
metrics: Automated metrics for 3D asset quality (not specified in detail)

results.value: Users preferred VFusion3D results over 90% of the time compared to current SOTA feed-forward 3D generative models

novelty_claims: First to leverage pre-trained video diffusion models as a scalable 3D data generator for training 3D generative models
Unlocking multi-view generative capabilities in video diffusion models through fine-tuning with 3D data
Demonstrating that synthetic multi-view data generated from video diffusion models can effectively train high-quality 3D generative models
Achieving state-of-the-art performance in feed-forward 3D asset generation from a single image, with significant user preference over existing methods

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["A novel diffusion-based pipeline (Vivid-ZOO) for Text-to-Multi-view-Video (T2MVid) generation","Factorization of T2MVid generation into viewpoint-space and time components","Reuse and combination of layers from pre-trained multi-view image diffusion models (e.g., MVdream) and 2D video diffusion models (e.g., AnimateDiff)","Introduction of 3D-2D alignment layers to align features to the latent space of pre-trained 2D temporal layers","Introduction of 2D-3D alignment layers to project features back to the multi-view space"],"problems":["Lack of methods for diffusion-based Text-to-Multi-view-Video (T2MVid) generation","Scarcity of large-scale captioned multi-view video datasets","Difficulty in modeling the complex, multi-dimensional distribution of multi-view videos (viewpoint, space, time)","Domain gap between synthetic multi-view image data and real-world 2D video data"],"datasets":["A newly constructed dataset of 14,271 captioned multi-view videos (introduced in this paper)","Multi-view image datasets (e.g., Objaverse, used for pre-training)","2D video datasets (e.g., WebVid10M, used for pre-training)"],"metrics":["Temporal coherence","Multi-view consistency","Generation quality (likely measured by FID, IS, or human evaluation, though not explicitly stated in the provided text)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First study to propose a diffusion-based pipeline for Text-to-Multi-view-Video (T2MVid) generation","First to combine and reuse layers from pre-trained 2D video and multi-view image diffusion models for T2MVid","Introduction of 3D-2D and 2D-3D alignment layers to bridge the domain gap and enable effective layer reuse","Ability to train a large-scale T2MVid diffusion model using only a small dataset of captioned multi-view videos","Contribution of a new captioned multi-view video dataset with text descriptions and camera poses"]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Multi-view diffusion model (MVDream) that generates consistent multi-view images from text prompts","Joint training on multi-view images (from 3D assets) and 2D image-text pairs","Application of Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) for 3D generation using the multi-view diffusion model","Transfer learning from pre-trained 2D diffusion models","DreamBooth-style fine-tuning for learning new concepts from a few 2D examples"],"problems":["Inconsistent multi-view generation in 2D-lifting methods for 3D generation (multi-face Janus issue, content drift across views)","Lack of 3D-awareness and multi-view knowledge in 2D diffusion models used for 3D generation","Limited generalizability and diversity in existing 3D object generation methods due to data scarcity and complexity"],"datasets":["Multi-view images from 3D assets (unspecified, but used for training)","2D image-text pairs (unspecified, but used for training)"],"metrics":["Multi-view consistency","Generalizability","Diversity"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a multi-view diffusion model that generates consistent multi-view images from text prompts, serving as a generalizable 3D prior agnostic to 3D representations","Jointly leverages 2D and 3D data to achieve both the generalizability of 2D diffusion models and the consistency of 3D renderings","Demonstrates that multi-view supervision during score distillation is significantly more stable than single-view 2D diffusion models for 3D generation","Enables DreamBooth-style concept learning for 3D generation from a few 2D examples, with robust multi-view consistency","Successfully generates 3D NeRF models without multi-view consistency issues, matching or surpassing the diversity of state-of-the-art methods"]}

methods: Multi-view diffusion model (MVDream) that generates consistent multi-view images from text prompts
Joint training on multi-view images (from 3D assets) and 2D image-text pairs
Application of Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) for 3D generation using the multi-view diffusion model
Transfer learning from pre-trained 2D diffusion models
DreamBooth-style fine-tuning for learning new concepts from a few 2D examples
problems: Inconsistent multi-view generation in 2D-lifting methods for 3D generation (multi-face Janus issue, content drift across views)

2D image-text pairs (unspecified, but used for training)
novelty_claims: Proposes a multi-view diffusion model that generates consistent multi-view images from text prompts, serving as a generalizable 3D prior agnostic to 3D representations
Jointly leverages 2D and 3D data to achieve both the generalizability of 2D diffusion models and the consistency of 3D renderings
Demonstrates that multi-view supervision during score distillation is significantly more stable than single-view 2D diffusion models for 3D generation
Enables DreamBooth-style concept learning for 3D generation from a few 2D examples, with robust multi-view consistency
Successfully generates 3D NeRF models without multi-view consistency issues, matching or surpassing the diversity of state-of-the-art methods

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Explicit injection of 3D priors from retrieved reference objects without retraining the 2D diffusion model","Keypoints supervision through a sparse ray sampling approach for 3D geometry","Modulation of 2D diffusion model output to match correct patterns of template views using a unified image adapter","Creative point growth and pruning during optimization to balance template resemblance and creative generation","Re-retrieval mechanism to update reference templates based on generated shapes"],"problems":["Inconsistent appearances in text-to-3D generation (e.g., faces on the back view)","Inaccurate 3D shapes (e.g., animals with extra legs) due to reliance on 2D diffusion supervision","Difficulty in balancing 2D generation quality with 3D consistency","High computational cost and quality degradation from retraining 2D diffusion models on rendered 3D data"],"datasets":["External 3D object datasets (not named specifically in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Multi-view consistency","Fidelity","Diversity"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to explicitly inject 3D priors from retrieved reference objects into text-to-3D generation without retraining the 2D diffusion model.","Proposes a sparse ray sampling method for keypoints supervision, enabling high-quality and diverse 3D geometry.","Decouples 3D geometry and appearance supervision, using template-based modulation to resolve appearance ambiguities while preserving style.","Enables creative point growth and pruning during optimization, allowing both accurate and creative 3D shape generation.","Introduces a re-retrieval mechanism to adaptively update reference templates based on generated shapes."]}

methods: Explicit injection of 3D priors from retrieved reference objects without retraining the 2D diffusion model
Keypoints supervision through a sparse ray sampling approach for 3D geometry
Modulation of 2D diffusion model output to match correct patterns of template views using a unified image adapter
Creative point growth and pruning during optimization to balance template resemblance and creative generation
Re-retrieval mechanism to update reference templates based on generated shapes
problems: Inconsistent appearances in text-to-3D generation (e.g., faces on the back view)

novelty_claims: First to explicitly inject 3D priors from retrieved reference objects into text-to-3D generation without retraining the 2D diffusion model.
Proposes a sparse ray sampling method for keypoints supervision, enabling high-quality and diverse 3D geometry.
Decouples 3D geometry and appearance supervision, using template-based modulation to resolve appearance ambiguities while preserving style.
Enables creative point growth and pruning during optimization, allowing both accurate and creative 3D shape generation.
Introduces a re-retrieval mechanism to adaptively update reference templates based on generated shapes.

methods: Ouroboros3D: a unified 3D generation framework integrating diffusion-based multi-view image generation and 3D reconstruction into a recursive diffusion process

3D-aware feedback mechanism: using 3D-aware maps rendered by the reconstruction module at the previous timestep as additional conditions during multi-view denoising

novelty_claims: First framework to integrate multi-view generation and 3D reconstruction into a recursive diffusion process with joint training

methods: IM-3D: Iterative Multiview Diffusion and Reconstruction
Fine-tuning Emu Video (a text-to-video generator) for multi-view generation
3D reconstruction using Gaussian Splatting (GS) with image-based loss (LPIPS)
Iterative feedback loop: feeding reconstructed 3D model back to the 2D generator for further refinement
problems: Inefficiency and instability of Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) in text-to-3D generation
Artifacts and geometric inconsistencies in 3D models generated from 2D generators
Lack of high-quality, consistent multi-view generation for 3D reconstruction
Need for large 3D reconstruction networks in previous approaches
datasets: Synthetic 3D assets (for fine-tuning Emu Video)
metrics: Quality of 3D output (e.g., LPIPS image-based loss)

results.metric: Number of 2D generator evaluations (IM-3D vs SDS)
results.value: 10-100x reduction (IM-3D requires ~40-80 evaluations vs tens of thousands for SDS)
Higher yield and better quality than SDS and previous methods (qualitative claim)

novelty_claims: First to leverage a text-to-video generator (Emu Video) for high-quality, consistent multi-view generation in text-to-3D tasks.
Demonstrates that fine-tuned video generators can directly produce up to 16 high-resolution, consistent views for 3D reconstruction.
Introduces an efficient pipeline that eliminates the need for distillation and large 3D reconstruction networks by using Gaussian Splatting and image-based losses.
Proposes an iterative feedback loop between 3D reconstruction and 2D generation, closing the loop only a few times per asset, greatly improving efficiency and quality.
Achieves state-of-the-art text/image-to-3D results with dramatically fewer 2D generator evaluations and faster reconstruction.

General algorithm for extracting smooth and textured meshes from 3D Gaussians.
Achieves high-resolution (512) 3D generation with fast feed-forward speed (~5 seconds), surpassing previous methods in both quality and efficiency.

methods: HiFi-123: a high-fidelity one-image-to-3D content generation method
Reference-Guided Novel View Enhancement (RGNV) pipeline based on depth-based DDIM inversion
Reference-Guided State Distillation (RGSD) loss for optimization-based image-to-3D generation

problems: Low fidelity and inconsistency in novel view synthesis from a single image using diffusion models
Blurry textures and deviations from the reference image in generated 3D content

novelty_claims: Introduction of the Reference-Guided Novel View Enhancement (RGNV) pipeline, which can be used as a plug-and-play module to improve fidelity in diffusion-based zero-shot novel view synthesis methods.
Development of the Reference-Guided State Distillation (RGSD) loss, which enhances optimization-based image-to-3D generation quality and achieves state-of-the-art performance.
Integration of depth information into DDIM inversion and sampling with depth-conditioned stable diffusion models for improved reconstruction quality.
Demonstration of significant improvements in both fidelity and consistency for single-image-to-3D generation compared to prior state-of-the-art methods.

methods: RenderDiffusion: a diffusion model for 3D generation and inference trained with only monocular 2D supervision
A novel image denoising architecture that generates and renders an intermediate 3D representation (triplane representation) at each denoising step
Use of a volumetric renderer to render the 3D representation back into a 2D image during denoising
problems: Lack of 3D-consistent generation and inference in image diffusion models
High memory demands and slow convergence of explicit 3D representations (e.g., voxels) in diffusion models

datasets: FFHQ
AFHQ
ShapeNet
CLEVR

results.value: Improved over state-of-the-art method [8] trained with only monocular supervision

novelty_claims: RenderDiffusion is the first diffusion model for 3D generation and inference trained using only monocular 2D supervision
Introduction of a denoising architecture that incorporates an explicit latent 3D representation (triplane) into the diffusion process
Ability to generate 3D-consistent scenes and perform 3D inference tasks (reconstruction, inpainting) without explicit 3D supervision
Avoids cubic memory growth of volumetric data by using triplane representation and only 2D supervision

methods: Generative 3D Gaussian Splatting for 3D content creation
Progressive densification of 3D Gaussians during optimization

results.metric: Generation time (single-view image to high-quality textured mesh)

novelty_claims: First to adapt 3D Gaussian Splatting to generative 3D content creation, enabling much faster optimization-based 2D lifting methods.
Proposes a new, efficient algorithm for mesh extraction from 3D Gaussians using local density querying.
Introduces a novel UV-space texture refinement stage with image space supervision for improved texture fidelity and efficiency.
Demonstrates that progressive densification of 3D Gaussians aligns well with generative optimization, leading to significant acceleration over NeRF-based methods.
